summary: SSL peer verification should reference stream->context options
description: Currently, the {{capture_peer_cert}} option is read before the stream is created (from {{options->ctx}}). We should move that between stream creation and crypto setup, and have it access {{stream->context}} instead. Additionally, {{php_stream_context_get_option()}} calls after crypto setup should check {{stream->context}} instead of {{options->ctx}}.

summary: explain() does not raise appropriate exception for $err conditions
description:
Run {{explain()}} on a query which has no candidate plans (enable {{notablescan}}) and the result is a {{$err}} document (presumably the server result document).

Previous versions, notably 1.5.4, raise an exception for the same condition.

summary: WiredTiger and listCollections issues?
description: https://groups.google.com/d/msg/mongodb-user/-B0rLAx9nQ0/djtXcsqD9jUJ

summary: Fix database name validation to include '$external', to allow execution of user admin commands
description:
See: http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/tutorial/control-access-to-mongodb-with-kerberos-authentication/#add-kerberos-principal-s-to-mongodb

Creating users for external auth mechanisms (e.g. Kerberos, LDAP) requires the ability to execute commands against the special {{$external}} database name. This is not possible given our current validation of database name arguments, as we throw an exception attempting to create a MongoDB instance with a {{$}} character.

Our current database validation is a mix of [Unix|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/limits/#Restrictions-on-Database-Names-for-Unix-and-Linux-Systems] and [Windows|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/limits/#Restrictions-on-Database-Names-for-Windows] naming restrictions, both of which disallow the dollar sign. A flexible solution would be to accept {{$external}} but still disallow other strings containing a dollar sign.

summary: MongoCollection::getIndexInfo on non-existing collection makes segmentation fault
MongoCollection::distinct() should ensure query is serialized as BSON object
MongoCollection::distinct() should allow array or object for query
description: Other methods that take a query parameter use "H" for {{zend_parse_parameters()}}. We should accept arrays/objects here, too, for consistency.

summary: MongoCollection::findAndModify() should allow array or object for query, update, and fields
description: Other methods that take a query parameter use "H" for {{zend_parse_parameters()}}. We should accept arrays/objects here, too, for consistency.

summary: bson_encode() does not work with objects that inherit MongoId
MongoCursorTimeoutException with MongoCursor->rewind() method
Connecting to replica set error - No candidate servers found
description:
I got error "No candidate servers found" under high load.
When many update queries, smth goes slow. Response on query can be 1, 2, 3,...60 secs, until connection timeout (I read this bug https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/PHP-927. And I do not explicitly specify connectTimeoutMS).

Connection hostnames:

{noformat}
mongodb://cs1xx32.xx.ru:27017,cs1xx33.xx.ru:27017,cs1xx96.xx.ru:27017/?replicaSet=rs0&readPreference=primaryPreferred
{noformat}

Replica set communicates over local network interface. So in /etc/hosts of each server we could see:

{noformat}
10.0.0.172  cs1xx96.xx.ru
10.0.0.170  cs1xx32.xx.ru
10.0.0.171  cs1xx33.xx.ru
{noformat}

(I tried over the internet as well — same issue)

Actually error messages are (7-10 per sec):

 * Couldn't get connection: No candidate servers found
 * Unable to connect to MongoDB server at MongoClient::connect(): php_network_getaddresses: getaddrinfo failed: Name or service not known.

MongoLog warns:

 * connection_create: error while creating connection for cs1xx96:27017: Connection timed out
 * Couldn't connect to 'cs1xx96:27017': Connection timed out
 * discover_topology: could not connect to new host: cs1xx96:27017: Previous connection attempts failed, server blacklisted

mongostats:

 * writelock ~4% on updated db
 * connections on primary ~40
 * connections on secondaries ~1000

rs.conf():

{

        ]
}
{noformat}

summary: php_mongo_stream_notify_io() segfault with stream context options
description:
First of all, apologies if I am missing anything, this is my first bug report here...

Connecting to a MongoDB instance using ssl breaks while using a non ssl version works fine with our unit tests (and prod code!)

This happens with both PHP 5.3 (1.5.1) and PHP 5.6 (1.6.7).

The driver is installed as rpm like this:
php-pecl-mongo.x86_64                  1.6.7-1.el6.remi.5.6      @remi-php56

We recently updated the openssl libraries, so my only guess atm is that that is related as it used to work on the old PHP version.

Unfortunately, I only do have a dump from PHP 5.6 and not an isolated test case.
MongoDB server is 2.4.13 using a self signed certificate, so our connection code looks something like this:

{code}
$options = array(
    'readPreference' => MongoClient::RP_PRIMARY,
    'w' => 1,
);
$driverOptions = array();
$driverOptions['context'] = stream_context_create(array(
    'ssl' => array(
        'verify_peer' => false,
        'verify_peer_name' => false,
    ),
));
$mongo = new MongoClient($server, $options, $driverOptions);
{code}

And here is the dump:

summary: MongoClient does not accept boolean false as "ssl" option
Log original string values for parsed boolean options
Ensure buffer lengths are checked in bson_to_zval()
Ensure DBPointer namespace length reads as little-endian
description: When parsing the {{ns_len}} from a DBPointer BSON type, the driver doesn't use the {{MONGO_32()}} macro, which handles endian conversion if the platform requires it. Even though DBPointer is a deprecated BSON type, we should still parse it consistently with other types.

summary: Ensure value is freed when bson_to_zval() encounters an error
description:
Currently, {{bson_to_zval()}} forgets to free its {{value}} zval in two places:

 * After recursing to decode a code scope and discovering that an exception was thrown
 * After encountering an unrecognized type and throwing an exception

summary: Ensure strings are null-terminated in bson_to_zval()
Ensure BSON buffer is checked before reading regex pattern/flags
description: Currently, {{bson_to_zval()}} only checks that we can read one null byte. We should handle these cstrings like BSON field names (i.e. check that we can read {{strlen()}} + 1 bytes).

summary: bson_decode() should require exactly one document in buffer
description:
While reviewing invalid BSON tests from the [Python driver|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver/blob/ff3c18ffbc05f0a39874faf25b896c71ff4ad009/test/test_bson.py#L162], we noted that buffers with extra, trailing data are considered invalid. 

Currently, {{bson_to_zval()}} returns after parsing a document and ignores any trailing data in the buffer string. While that behavior is reasonable for cursors, where iteration repeatedly invokes the function on its larger buffer (derived from the OP_REPLY message), we probably don't want to do that with {{bson_decode()}}. Instead, {{bson_decode()}} should ensure that the buffer it receives is exactly one document.

summary: Query with limit leaves open cursors on server
Fix static compilation
description:
i use "./configure --enable-mongo" to static compile, failed...

error message:
"
ext/mongo/contrib/.libs/crypto.o: In function `php_hash_copy':
/root/php-5.6.10/ext/mongo/contrib/crypto.c:25: multiple definition of `php_hash_copy'
ext/hash/.libs/hash.o:/root/php-5.6.10/ext/hash/hash.c:116: first defined here
collect2: ld returned 1 exit status
make: *** [sapi/cli/php] Error 1
"

summary: Can't install driver on PHP 5.2
description:
I just tried to install php-mongo drive on a php 5.2 and got this error while running make..
Any idea?
/usr/local/src/mongo-php-driver/php_mongo.c:102: warning: initialization discards qualifiers from pointer target type
/usr/local/src/mongo-php-driver/php_mongo.c: In function ‘zm_globals_ctor_mongo’:
/usr/local/src/mongo-php-driver/php_mongo.c:367: error: ‘empty_fcall_info’ undeclared (first use in this function)

summary: GridFS should not drop dupes when creating index
description:
GridFS ensure index for some reason is creating unique indexes, with dropDupes.

I don't think dropDupes does anything anymore, but in 2.6 and earlier it definitely does.

summary: Database name too long with valid database name
description:
When I tried to use following string as connection url:

mongodb://heroku_XXXXXXXX:XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX@XXXXXXXX.mongolab.com:55782/heroku_XXXXXXXX

I received an error:

[MongoException]                                                                                                                             
  Database name cannot exceed 63 characters: CONNECTION URL

summary: PHP driver on big endian platform not connecting to MongoDB
description:
I built the PHP MongoDB driver on a Linux Z system (big endian) but have not been able to get it to connect to MongoDB.

When i invoke the MongoClient constructor in PHP, to create a connection, the MongoDB server reports "recv(): message len 1040187392 is invalid."
The value 1040187392 turns out to be hex 3E000000 i.e. would have value 62 (0x3E) with endianess reversed.

I also built the php MongoDB driver on a x86 machine (little endian) and it connects successfully to the same MongoDB server that was used above.

I ran tshark (wireshark) on the db server and it reported activity when trying to connect from both Linux Z and x86 systems. When connecting from x86 it reported a "MONGO" message with "Len=62" when the connection is made, but did not recognise any "MONGO" message when connecting from the Linux Z system.

Hence I seem to have an endianess problem with the PHP driver on the Linux Z system.

I installed the php driver using a manual source compile and install on each system ('phpize', './configure', 'make', 'sudo make install').

'./configure' reported the following:
On Z system "checking whether byte ordering is bigendian... yes"
On x86 system "checking whether byte ordering is bigendian... no"

Please could you suggest a fix for this problem.

Thanks

summary: PHP drivers seems to lag when connecting to a Replica Set
description:
Hi,

We are using the PHP driver with a replica set composed of two data bearing nodes and an arbiter.
Our application is on the same server as the primary server of the set and is set to "RP_PRIMARY".

In that config (PHP driver having the replicaset in the config), the app is slow. I mean that every query to the database takes 3 seconds to be sent to the mongo instance. As a result, it takes 3 seconds to open each page that needs to query the database. 

When we switch to a standalone config in the PHP driver (MongoDB is still running as a replica set), this 3 second lag totally disappears and the app is blazing fast.

Replica set PHP config :
{code}
'server'           => 'server1.com,server2.com',
'port'             => '27017',
'user'             => 'username',
'password'         => 'password',
'dbname'           => 'main',
'options'          => array(
    'authMechanism'     => 'SCRAM-SHA-1',
    'authSource'        => 'admin',
    'timeout'          => -1,
    'connectTimeoutMS' => 300000, // 5 minutes
    'socketTimeoutMS'  => 300000, // 5 minutes
    'readPreference'   => \MongoClient::RP_PRIMARY,
    'replicaSet'      => 'replicaset',
)
{code}

Standalone PHP config :
{code}
'server'           => 'server1.com',
'port'             => '27017',
'user'             => 'username',
'password'         => 'password',
'dbname'           => 'main',
'options'          => array(
    'authMechanism'     => 'SCRAM-SHA-1',
    'authSource'        => 'admin',
    'timeout'          => -1,
    'connectTimeoutMS' => 300000, // 5 minutes
    'socketTimeoutMS'  => 300000, // 5 minutes
)
{code}

I don't know if this is coming from a bad config on our end, or if this is global.

Tell me if you need any other information.
Bastien Diederichs

summary: We don't allocate enough space for the hash buffer
description: A user on IRC contacted me to say they have a crash. After him being very helpful during a live debugging I figured out that we fail to allocate space for the "-;" and ".;" in the hash in the cases there is no replset name, or username/password/db combination. That combined with a PID that is *more* than 5 characters (we reserve 10 for it), caused a valgrind warning because there was not enough space for the "\0" any more.

summary: Driver does not remember when a member of a connection pool is down
description:
Hello,

I have this situation :
One of my connection member is down (the server is down not only the mongo process)
My php over apache uses this connection pattern to connect :
mongodb://user:password@mongos1:27020,mongos2:27020
or
mongodb://user:password@member1:27017,member2:27017/rs1

I set the connectTimeoutMS to 1000 
(I think that letting the default behavior to 60000 ms is a very bad idea, because when you have a member down, all apache workers get stuck ...)

But if one of my members goes down, everytime I create a new MongoClient it takes one seconde to determine that the host is down.

That means that when one of my servers goes down, all my web services are slowed down.

Why the driver does not remember that a server is down and blacklist it for a while? Why does it have to retry to connect to it and lock the process everytime we allocate a new MongoClient ?

I haven't find a workaround to fix this. Do you have an idea how to deal with it ?

Thanks for your help,

Thomas.

summary: when connect the db then gives error as "Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'Failed to connect to: localhost:27017: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it. '
MongoCommandCursor::timeout() not working

MongoCommandCursor hasn't timeout method, so the only way to change timeout is using deprecated \MongoCursor::$timeout = -1;
*my query is*:-

{noformat}
$options = array("maxTimeMS" => 120000);
$cursor = $this->aggregateCursor(array(
	array('$match' => $condition),
	array('$project' => $project),
	array('$group' => array(
		'_id' => $group,

	)),
), $options );

summary: Socket timeout not applied to initial command cursor query
description:
Given the following:

{code}
$mongo = new \MongoClient( 'localhost/mydb', array(
  'connect' => true,
));
$collection = $mongo->selectCollection( 'mydb', 'mycollection' );
$pipeline = ... ;
$cursor = $collection->aggregateCursor( $pipeline );
$cursor->timeout( 120000 );
$cursor->rewind(); // exception here
{code}

I get the following exception: {{exception 'MongoCursorTimeoutException' with message 'localhost:27017: Read timed out after reading 0 bytes, waited for 30.000000 seconds' in ...}}

I've enabled MongoLog just before the query using the following code:

{code}
\MongoLog::setLevel(\MongoLog::ALL);
\MongoLog::setModule(\MongoLog::ALL);
\MongoLog::setCallback(function($module, $level, $message)
{
  static $logFile = false;

  if (!$logFile) {
    $logFile = "/storage/MONGO-PHP-LOG." . time();
    error_log('REQUEST_URI: ' . $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'], 3, $logFile);
  }

  switch ($module) {
    case \MongoLog::RS: $module = "REPLSET"; break;
    case \MongoLog::CON: $module = "CON"; break;
    case \MongoLog::IO: $module = "IO"; break;
    case \MongoLog::SERVER: $module = "SERVER"; break;
    case \MongoLog::PARSE: $module = "PARSE"; break;
  }

  switch ($level) {
    case \MongoLog::WARNING: $level = "WARN"; break;
    case \MongoLog::INFO: $level = "INFO"; break;
    case \MongoLog::FINE: $level = "FINE"; break;
    default: return $level;
  }

  $log = sprintf("%s :: %s (%s): %s\n", date('Y-m-d H:i:s',time()), $module, $level, $message);

  error_log($log, 3, $logFile);
});
{code}

summary: GridFS file storage fails on big endian platform
description:
I built the PHP MongoDB driver on a Linux Z system (big endian) but was not able to get it to store a file to MongoDB.

When I invoked gridfs storeFile the program ran out of memory.

I found that it was due to the chunk_size parameter having a value of zero, causing an infinite loop when reading the file.

chunk_size seems to be handled in an inconsistent way. Initialised as a Long but read as an Int.

By default it is initialised to 261120, which when stored as a Long would be read as zero if seen as an Int (on big endian), but read as 261120 if seen as an Int (on little endian).

Changing the declaration of the chunk_size variable from Int to Long appears to fix the problem on big endian and not cause an issue on little endian (previously passing tests provided with the driver continue to pass).

The change affects php_mongo.c only. Diff of the change for release 1.6.11 as follows:-
503c503
<       int chunk_size;
---
>       long chunk_size;

Please could you fix this issue.

summary: Segfault on node with high network latency

#9  0x00007f938cd38bde in mongo_get_read_write_connection (manager=0x1a44e50, servers=0x1b9fff0, connection_flags=9, error_message=error_message@entry=0x7fff4b4c3df8)
    at /tmp/pear/temp/mongo/mcon/manager.c:553
#10 0x00007f938cd0f8d2 in php_mongo_connect (link=<optimized out>, flags=<optimized out>) at /tmp/pear/temp/mongo/mongoclient.c:357
#11 0x00007f938cd100ae in php_mongo_ctor (ht=<optimized out>, return_value=<optimized out>, return_value_ptr=<optimized out>, this_ptr=<optimized out>, return_value_used=<optimized out>,
    bc=<optimized out>) at /tmp/pear/temp/mongo/mongoclient.c:571
{noformat}

I trying to debug and think this caused by some race condition in mongo_connection recycling.

In filter_connections i added mongo_manager_log before mongo_server_hash_to_pid and found that on every segfault we got "broken" mongo_connection memory for our node with high network latency:

{noformat}
PHP Notice:  REPLSET WARN: filter_connections: con:ping_ms 117835526, con:connection_type 117835526, con:connected 117835526, con::hash (null) ; current pid (29226)

PHP Notice:  REPLSET WARN: filter_connections: con:ping_ms 4, con:connection_type 16, con:connected 1, con::hash (null) ; current pid (22912) 

PHP Notice:  REPLSET WARN: filter_connections: con:ping_ms 12, con:connection_type 30379568, con:connected 0, con::hash (null) ; current pid (23825)
{noformat}

Disabling this node, fixed this issue.

summary: Route unacknowledged writes through legacy op codes
append_getlasterror() should ignore unset fsync/journal from link
Can't run the test suite against MongoDB 3.1.2 or later
description:
MongoDB 3.1.2 removed `startMongod` which we use to startup MongoDB.

This needs to be replaced with MongoRunner.runMongod

summary: Specifying a persist option to the PHP driver constructor without any other options causes Apache to segfault.
description:
Specifying a persist option to the PHP driver constructor without any other options causes Apache to segfault.

All I had to do to reproduce this was specify the 'persist' option. The 'persist' option did work with a 'replicaSet' option, though.

summary: Map-reduce on sharded collections creates documents on all shards
Extension should link OpenSSL during compilation
description:
Although the extension conditionally includes libcrypto headers at build time if the OpenSSL extension is available, it does not use {{PHP_SETUP_OPENSSL()}} to link to it. This could lead to undefined symbol errors if PHP itself does not happen to load OpenSSL at runtime.

Original description:

bq. Pecl extension doesn't load when using PHP 5.6.31.  I have confirmed the mongo.so exists and is included in the loaded php.ini file. I see no mention of any issues in the logs. We have no issues if we spin up another box using php version 5.6.28

summary: Option for retrieving documents as object rather then arrays
description: We need an option, similar to $assoc=bool in json_decode() (http://php.net/json_decode) to be able to give the user the chance of decoding the documents as objects or an array
